title: Rosy Chewy Egyptian Cassava Flatbread
description: Winey, chewy, light Egyptian flatbread made with cassava flour and a lamination‑puff technique, low in calories and vegan.
instructions: In a bowl whisk the cassava flour, salt, sugar, and yeast until well combined.
Add the water, wine, and olive oil and stir until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough, let it rise in a warm place for 30 minutes until doubled in size.
Press the dough flat and brush the surface with olive oil.
Fold the dough into thirds and roll it out again, repeating the fold and roll three times to create lamination layers.
Cut the dough into 6 equal rounds and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment.
Bake at 200°C for 12 minutes until the flatbreads puff and develop a rosy tint.
Cool on a rack, slice, and serve warm or at room temperature.
